regulators.role: Independently determines tax appeals and statutory references that legislation assigns to the First-tier Tribunal.
powers: Determine a tax appeal or payment-postponement reference only where an enactment gives the tribunal jurisdiction.
Make the outcome permitted by the legal provision governing the exact appeal or reference.
limits: It is not HMRC and does not administer charity tax relief or issue tax returns and assessments.
Its jurisdiction is decision-specific; a disagreement, correction or determination is not appealable merely because another tax event is.
This supplement does not map onward Upper Tribunal or court appeals.

officialProcedures.name: Trust notification of Income Tax or Capital Gains Tax chargeability under TMA 1970 section 7

evidence.locator: Current consolidated provision and TaxSorted fail-closed procedure synthesis
TaxSorted exact treatment, selector, institution and rule mapping over the complete cited provision set; not clause-level attribution
TaxSorted analysis labels and possible-next-door synthesis over the complete cited provision set; not a mandatory sequence or clause-level attribution

officialProcedures.trigger: The trustees are chargeable to Income Tax or Capital Gains Tax for a tax year and no relevant trustee-return notice has been given, or a notice has been withdrawn, subject to every exception and condition in section 7.
steps: Identify the trustees who are the legal taxpayer and the tax and tax year concerned.
Check whether HMRC gave or withdrew a notice requiring a trustee return.
If section 7 applies, the trustees notify HMRC of chargeability; that notification is not the trustee return.
officialProcedures.timeLimit: Where no trustee-return notice was received, the notification period is six months from the tax-year end. Where a notice was withdrawn, it ends on the later of that six-month point and 30 days beginning with the day after withdrawal; the actual tax-year end and withdrawal facts are required.
officialProcedures.paymentEffect: Notification does not itself assess tax, set a payment date or postpone recovery.
possibleOutcomes: The trustees notify HMRC of chargeability.
No section 7 notification duty is established because an exception applies or a required selector is missing.
HMRC may separately issue a trustee-return notice.
doesNotProve: That the trust has lost a charity exemption or owes tax.
That HMRC issued a return notice.
That the notification is an appealable decision.
That nextProcedureIds form a mandatory, complete or universal sequence.

officialProcedures.name: Trustee return and self-assessment under TMA 1970 sections 8A and 9

evidence.locator: Current consolidated provision and TaxSorted fail-closed procedure synthesis
Additional exact current primary-law provision used by this procedure
TaxSorted exact treatment, selector, institution and rule mapping over the complete cited provision set; not clause-level attribution
TaxSorted analysis labels and possible-next-door synthesis over the complete cited provision set; not a mandatory sequence or clause-level attribution

officialProcedures.trigger: HMRC gives the relevant trustees a notice under section 8A requiring a trustee return for the identified tax year.
steps: Retain the actual section 8A notice, its date, the covered tax year and the trustees to whom it was addressed.
The trustees deliver the required return and include their own self-assessment under section 9.
Treat the filing deadline supplied for the case separately from any payment deadline.
officialProcedures.timeLimit: The filing date depends on the actual notice and statutory branch. This record requires the filing deadline and return-filing date rather than inferring a date from the tax year alone.
officialProcedures.paymentEffect: A trustee self-assessment can identify tax payable, but sections 8A and 9 do not replace the separate payment-timing rules.
possibleOutcomes: A trustee return containing the trustees' self-assessment is filed.
A required return remains absent, which may open the separate section 28C determination door.
No filing conclusion is returned because the notice, period or dates are missing.
doesNotProve: That the trustees' own self-assessment is an HMRC decision carrying a generic appeal right.
That a charity exemption fails or the return is incorrect.
That the filing date and payment date are the same.
That nextProcedureIds form a mandatory, complete or universal sequence.

officialProcedures.trigger: The trustees seek to amend their own trustee return within the amendment power and time conditions in section 9ZA.
steps: Identify the exact return and its statutory filing date.
Check the proposed amendment date against the section 9ZA window.

transparencyGaps.title: No reported-case holding is applied without a safely matched official decision
transparencyGaps.detail: This supplement maps current primary-law procedure but does not admit the applicability or effect of any reported decision. No exact official judgment was established as safely controlling a charity's facts, tax, period, document and procedural posture in this bounded review.
transparencyGaps.consequence: The API may expose statutory gateways and an explicit case-law gap, but must not claim that a discovery, assessment, appeal or tribunal outcome is valid or invalid by importing an unmatched case summary.

transparencyGaps.title: Three territorial recovery branches are admitted, not the full recovery system
transparencyGaps.detail: The supplement does not map court debt proceedings, direct recovery from bank or building-society accounts, insolvency, winding up, bankruptcy, sequestration, judicial review, injunctions or downstream appeals. It also stops before the detailed England and Wales notice and execution rules and the optional Northern Ireland forced-entry warrant branch, and does not resolve interaction with restricted charity property, trustees' duties or charity-regulator powers.
transparencyGaps.consequence: Consumers must not treat the three admitted territorial records as HMRC's only remedies or infer that an omitted court, bank, insolvency or public-law route is unavailable.

transparencyGaps.title: Composite procedure records cite their provision sets but do not yet expose clause-level attribution
transparencyGaps.detail: The affected records identify every admitted provision and expose valid record-level JSON-pointer evidence. Some scalar fields and list fields synthesize more than one provision, while their evidence entries do not yet identify the exact sentence, list item, subsection or paragraph supplied by each provision. Their provenance is therefore accurate at the cited provision-set level, not at clause level.
transparencyGaps.consequence: Consumers may show the complete cited provision set and the bounded synthesis, but must not state that a lead source independently supports every word of a composite field or present the current pointers as clause-level attribution. That claim requires split fields or list items and exact subsection or paragraph locators.
